purpose: Single source of truth for comparing SDK/framework telemetry coverage
changelog: 1.2.1 (2026-06-17): Add VR-021 (conversation_thread view rule): gen_ai.conversation.id (AR-041) is set by AgentTelemetryLayer on invoke_agent spans only; ChatTelemetryLayer does not forward options to _get_span_attributes so inner chat spans never carry this attribute. Add span_scope note to AR-041. Update AR-041 note in attributes.

1.2.0 (2026-06-16): Add dashboard-derived attributes: gen_ai.client.token.usage metric (AR-044); gen_ai.prompt.caching metric (AR-045); gen_ai.guardrail.grounding_type dimension (AR-046); span.status_code (AR-047); gen_ai.model bigevent field (AR-048); event.type generic gate (AR-049). Add audit_trail and service_health view_rules (VR-016..VR-019). Add kong_gateway_metrics section (KO-001..KO-004) and nvidia_nim_metrics section (NV-001..NV-008). Note AR-020/AR-021 not yet visualised in any dashboard.

1.1.0 (2026-06-12): Add gen_ai.token.type (AR-024); add gen_ai.client.operation.duration (AR-025); add legacy token fallback attributes (AR-026, AR-027); add evaluation subsystem (AR-028..AR-040); add gen_ai.bedrock.guardrail.contextual (AR-041); add gen_ai.conversation.id (AR-042); add gen_ai.request.temperature (AR-043); add gen_ai.system_instructions (AR-044); fix normalization.legacy_fallbacks direction (primary→legacy, not legacy→primary).

1.1.1 (2026-06-12): Revert provider priority: gen_ai.system is deprecated in favor of gen_ai.provider.name per OTel semconv. The DQL coalesce(gen_ai.system, gen_ai.provider.name) checks the old field first for backward compatibility only, not because it is preferred.

generic.description: Provider-agnostic profile. Covers all attributes in the all_genai_views, prompts_view, cost_dashboard, latency_charts, and service_health view_rules. Used for any OTel-compliant SDK regardless of provider.

openai.description: OpenAI provider profile. Includes all generic checks plus OpenAI prompt-caching attributes.

azure.description: Azure OpenAI provider profile. Includes all generic checks plus Azure content filter attributes.

bedrock.description: AWS Bedrock provider profile. Includes all generic checks plus Bedrock guardrail, caching, and contextual grounding attributes.

google.description: Google Gemini / Vertex AI provider profile. Includes all generic checks; no additional provider-specific guardrail attributes required.

nvidia.description: NVIDIA NIM provider profile. The dashboard uses NIM Prometheus metrics (NV-001..NV-008) alongside OTel spans from the NIM OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

kong.description: Kong AI Gateway profile. The dashboard is entirely metrics-driven (KO-001..KO-004 Prometheus metrics); it does not require OTel span attributes. The generic span checks apply only to the service-health tile that fetches spans alongside Kong metrics.

openinference-basic.reason: Basic OpenInference sample uses OpenAI-compatible chat flow, not Bedrock/Azure guardrail provider paths.

rules: Run provider-specific checks only when the scenario explicitly targets that provider profile.
Do not run Bedrock checks for openinference-basic unless provider is Bedrock.
For kong and nvidia profiles, skip span-based must_have_all checks that cannot be satisfied from metrics alone; validate provider_metrics separately.

notes: Use service.name instead of k8s.deployment.name in GH runner tests.
Provider-specific checks only apply when that provider profile is under test.
Do not fail generic SDK tests for Dynatrace enrichment fields (dt.*).
must_have_all for gen_ai.usage.input_tokens is satisfied by gen_ai.usage.prompt_tokens (see equivalents_any_of).
must_have_all for gen_ai.usage.output_tokens is satisfied by gen_ai.usage.completion_tokens (see equivalents_any_of).

rules.description: gen_ai.system is the original/legacy attribute, deprecated in OTel semconv in favor of gen_ai.provider.name. The DQL coalesce(gen_ai.system, gen_ai.provider.name) checks gen_ai.system first only for backward compatibility with older SDKs still emitting it. Prefer gen_ai.provider.name in new instrumentation.
Prefer modern input token field; use legacy prompt_tokens only as fallback.
Prefer modern output token field; use legacy completion_tokens only as fallback.
Prefer structured input messages; use legacy prompt.0.content as fallback.
Prefer structured output messages; use legacy completion.0.content as fallback.

all_genai_views.description: Base inclusion rule for GenAI spans across the app.

rules.note: gen_ai.system listed second — it is the deprecated fallback. DQL checks gen_ai.system first in coalesce for backward compat only.

prompts_view.description: Prompt list/detail queries with prompt/completion rendering.

overview_prompts_count.description: Overview prompt count query using the same request-type narrowing as prompts.

cost_dashboard.description: Token-based cost breakdown charts. Requires token counts AND token type to split input/output cost lanes.

rules.effect: missing_all → cost dashboard shows $0 with no warning
missing → input/output cost lanes cannot be separated

latency_charts.description: p99 and mean latency charts across provider/model dimensions.

rules.effect: missing → all latency charts empty with no error shown

evaluation_results.description: Evaluation result tab: pass/fail verdicts, LLM-as-judge scores, per-evaluation dedup.

rules.note: Evaluation data arrives as bizevents, not spans.

rules.effect: missing → verdict column blank, pass/fail summary unavailable

service_health.description: Success/error split used in the health tile of every provider dashboard. Derived from the standard OTel span status field, not a gen_ai attribute.

rules.effect: missing → all spans counted as successful; no error signal in health tile

audit_trail.description: Audit trail view driven by gen_ai.auditing bizevents. Independent of the span pipeline — data arrives via a separate bigevent stream.

rules.note: Audit trail data arrives as bizevents, not spans. SDK must emit gen_ai.auditing events separately.

rules.effect: missing → audit events cannot be grouped or filtered by model

conversation_thread.description: Conversation thread grouping in the prompts table. gen_ai.conversation.id groups spans into threads. In agent-framework SDKs the attribute is typically set only on the invoke_agent span (AgentTelemetryLayer passes all_options to _get_span_attributes); the inner chat span does not receive it because ChatTelemetryLayer does not pass the options dict. Thread grouping therefore depends on which span type the dashboard DQL queries.

rules.effect: absent_from_chat_span → prompts table conversation thread grouping is empty if DQL queries chat spans only; works if DQL queries invoke_agent spans or traverses the parent span hierarchy

bedrock_cache_tiles.description: Bedrock prompt-caching tiles that track token savings from cache reads/writes. Uses gen_ai.prompt.caching metric (distinct from gen_ai.prompt_caching span attribute AR-022).

rules.note: Metric used as timeseries sum(gen_ai.prompt.caching) filtered by gen_ai.cache.type == 'read' or 'write'. Distinct from gen_ai.prompt_caching (AR-022) which is a span attribute denoting cache hit/miss.
rules.effect: missing → cache savings tiles empty; cost-of-cache tiles show $0

rules.note: Dimension on gen_ai.bedrock.guardrail.contextual (AR-040) metric. Separates grounding score from relevance score tiles in the Bedrock dashboard.
rules.effect: missing → grounding and relevance scores cannot be split; both shown as a single undifferentiated contextual score

attributes.name: gen_ai.system
attributes.note: Legacy attribute, deprecated in OTel semconv in favor of gen_ai.provider.name. Still accepted by the app's any-of gate for backward compatibility.

attributes.name: gen_ai.usage.input_tokens
attributes.note: Satisfied by gen_ai.usage.prompt_tokens (AR-026) when absent — see equivalents_any_of.

attributes.name: gen_ai.usage.output_tokens
attributes.note: Satisfied by gen_ai.usage.completion_tokens (AR-027) when absent — see equivalents_any_of.

attributes.name: gen_ai.operation.name
attributes.note: Not always required by app filters but useful for SDK operation-level validation.

attributes.name: gen_ai.agent.name
attributes.note: Used in if(isNotNull(gen_ai.agent.name), 'Agent', else: 'LLM') to classify spans.

attributes.name: gen_ai.prompt.0.content
attributes.note: Removed from OTel semconv (gen_ai.prompt family removed with no direct replacement). Retained in baseline as a fallback for older SDKs that still emit it; prefer gen_ai.input.messages.

attributes.name: gen_ai.completion.0.content
attributes.note: Removed from OTel semconv (gen_ai.completion family removed with no direct replacement). Retained in baseline as a fallback for older SDKs that still emit it; prefer gen_ai.output.messages.

attributes.name: gen_ai.token.type
attributes.note: Required to split cost dashboard into input vs output cost lanes. 'cache_read' and 'cache_creation' are additional OTel semconv values for prompt-caching token accounting. Missing causes silent failure — cost shows as undifferentiated total.

attributes.name: gen_ai.client.operation.duration
attributes.note: Used for p99 and mean latency charts. Missing causes all latency views to be empty with no error surfaced.

attributes.name: gen_ai.evaluation.name
attributes.note: Part of dedup key ['span_id', 'gen_ai.evaluation.name'].

attributes.name: gen_ai.evaluation.score.label
attributes.note: Drives the pass/fail verdict expression.

attributes.name: gen_ai.evaluation.result
attributes.note: Value of event.type field for evaluation bizevents — gates the evaluation_results view (VR-013).

attributes.name: gen_ai.bedrock.guardrail.contextual
attributes.note: Contextual grounding score — distinct from the binary activation/content/topics/words guardrail counters.

attributes.name: gen_ai.conversation.id

span_scope.chat: absent — ChatTelemetryLayer does not pass the options dict to _get_span_attributes

attributes.note: Used to group spans into conversation threads in the prompts table. In agent-framework SDKs, this attribute is typically set only on the invoke_agent span by AgentTelemetryLayer; the inner chat (LLM) span does not carry it. See VR-021 for span-scope verification rule.

attributes.name: gen_ai.request.temperature
attributes.note: Display-only metadata — no filtering depends on this field.

attributes.name: gen_ai.system_instructions
attributes.note: System-level prompt shown alongside user messages in the prompts detail view.

attributes.name: gen_ai.client.token.usage
attributes.note: OTel metric (counter) that accumulates token counts. Used in timeseries queries: timeseries sum(gen_ai.client.token.usage) filtered by gen_ai.token.type dimension. Missing causes all cost dashboard tiles to show $0 with no error. Distinct from the span attributes gen_ai.usage.input_tokens/output_tokens (AR-006/AR-007) which carry per-span counts.

attributes.name: gen_ai.prompt.caching
attributes.note: Distinct from gen_ai.prompt_caching (AR-022), which is a span attribute denoting a cache hit. gen_ai.prompt.caching is a metric used as timeseries sum(gen_ai.prompt.caching) filtered by gen_ai.cache.type to count cache-read and cache-write tokens for cost-savings calculation.

attributes.name: gen_ai.guardrail.grounding_type
attributes.note: Dimension on the gen_ai.bedrock.guardrail.contextual (AR-040) metric. Separates grounding score (factual accuracy) from relevance score (query relevance) in the Bedrock contextual grounding tiles. Missing causes both scores to be aggregated into a single undifferentiated contextual value.

attributes.name: span.status_code
attributes.note: Standard OTel span status attribute used across every provider dashboard for the success/error health tile. Not a gen_ai attribute — emitted automatically by OTel SDKs. Missing causes all requests to appear successful.

attributes.name: gen_ai.model
attributes.note: Model identifier field on gen_ai.auditing bizevents. Used to group and filter audit events by model. Different from gen_ai.request.model (AR-004) and gen_ai.response.model (AR-005), which are span attributes.

attributes.name: event.type
attributes.note: Primary gate field for all bizevents processed by the app. Each bigevent stream has its own event.type value; the app filters on this before reading any other bigevent fields.

dashboard_gaps.note: Attributes defined in the baseline that are not yet visualised in any dashboard tile. These are emitted by the SDK but have no corresponding dashboard query.

unvisualised.name: gen_ai.bedrock.guardrail.topics
unvisualised.note: Attribute is expected from the Bedrock SDK but no dashboard tile queries it. The bedrock dashboard uses gen_ai.bedrock.guardrail.content (AR-018) for the 'toxic content' tile instead.

unvisualised.name: gen_ai.bedrock.guardrail.words
unvisualised.note: Attribute is expected from the Bedrock SDK but no dashboard tile queries it.

kong_gateway_metrics.name: kong_ai_llm_requests_total
kong_gateway_metrics.description: Total AI LLM requests routed through Kong AI Gateway, split by provider and model.

kong_gateway_metrics.name: kong_ai_llm_tokens_total
kong_gateway_metrics.description: Total token consumption through Kong AI Gateway. token_type dimension maps to prompt_tokens/completion_tokens/total_tokens (different naming from OTel gen_ai.token.type).

kong_gateway_metrics.name: kong_ai_llm_provider_latency_ms
kong_gateway_metrics.description: End-to-end latency (ms) for LLM provider calls as measured by Kong AI Gateway.

kong_gateway_metrics.name: kong_kong_latency_ms
kong_gateway_metrics.description: Kong gateway processing latency (ms), split by service and route.

nvidia_nim_metrics.name: e2e_request_latency_seconds
nvidia_nim_metrics.description: End-to-end request latency (seconds) for NIM inference requests.

nvidia_nim_metrics.name: request_success_total
nvidia_nim_metrics.description: Total number of successful NIM inference requests.

nvidia_nim_metrics.name: request_prompt_tokens
nvidia_nim_metrics.description: Total input (prompt) tokens processed by the NIM server.

nvidia_nim_metrics.name: request_generation_tokens
nvidia_nim_metrics.description: Total output (generation) tokens produced by the NIM server.

nvidia_nim_metrics.name: time_to_first_token_seconds
nvidia_nim_metrics.description: Time from request receipt to first generated token (seconds) — key streaming latency metric.

nvidia_nim_metrics.name: generation_tokens_total
nvidia_nim_metrics.description: Cumulative generation tokens produced, split by model. Used for throughput (rate: 1s) charts.

nvidia_nim_metrics.name: gpu_cache_usage_perc
nvidia_nim_metrics.description: GPU KV-cache utilisation percentage. High values indicate memory pressure; affects batching capacity.

nvidia_nim_metrics.name: num_requests_running
nvidia_nim_metrics.description: Number of inference requests currently in-flight on the NIM server.
